subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear articulation of the aim with a focused research question
Logical sectioning into introduction, methodology, results, discussion, and conclusion
Consistent use of signposting transitions to guide the reader
weaknesses: Inconsistent terminology (e.g. âBrazil GPâ vs âSÃ£o Paulo Grand Prixâ)
Lengthy F1 background and Netflix anecdote distract from the mathematical narrative
Dense paragraphs lack sub-headings or bullets, reducing readability
Bibliography entries vary in style and completeness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed (band 3) with a clear structure, labelled figures, and signposted transitions. However, recurring digressions, verbosity, and some organizational lapses prevent it from reaching the highest level of conciseness and polish. This mix of solid coherence and notable irrelevancies justifies a score of 3 out of 4.

strengths: Consistent and mostly correct use of statistical notation (mean, median, IQR, correlation)
Effective employment of multiple representations: tables, scatter plots with regression lines, box-and-whisker diagrams
Good logical flow in explanations, with interpretive statements tied to calculations
weaknesses: Inconsistent timeâformat notation (MM.SS vs SS.MM) between tables and text
Abrupt or incomplete rationales for methodological choices (e.g. linear regression)
Occasional LaTeX/formatting lapses and missing regression equations on figures
Minor misreferences (swapping driver names) and insufficient explanation of axis swaps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used appropriately and mostly consistently; the variety and clarity of representations support the argument, and the narrative has good logical structure. Minor notation inconsistencies and incomplete explanations prevent it from achieving the top band. A score of 3 out of 4 reflects this strong but imperfect communication.

strengths: Investigation motivated by personal lockdown F1 viewing habits, demonstrating clear personal voice
Use of raw FIA data and conversion routines shows independent decisionâmaking
Creation of bespoke visuals (e.g. comparative scatter plot) reflects initiative
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques remain standard and no novel model was developed
Approach, while personal, does not extend beyond typical statistical methods
Limited innovative extension or exploration beyond the core question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is significant evidence of independent thinking and a clear personal approach, and the student took initiative in data gathering and presentation. However, the techniques are standard and the investigation lacks outstanding originality. This aligns with band 2 for Personal Engagement.

strengths: Acknowledgment of singleârace limitations and fanâbias potential
Thoughtful discussion of confounders such as fuel burn and tyre wear
Insightful comments on outlier treatment (e.g. exclusion of lap 1) and variability measures
weaknesses: No explicit criteria for anomaly or safetyâcar lap exclusion were defined
Evaluation stops short of proposing detailed future work or methodological refinements
Some misleading interpretations (e.g. equating IQR equality with overall performance parity)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection, thoughtful analysis of confounders and data quality, and a clear evaluation of outcomes. However, the reflection lacks the depth, critical rigor, and comprehensive future-oriented suggestions needed for the highest band. A score of 2 out of 3 is therefore appropriate.

strengths: All mathematical procedures (unit conversion, regression, summary statistics) are correctly executed
Interpretations of slopes, r-values, means, medians, and IQRs demonstrate thorough understanding
Use of official FIA data ensures accuracy and reproducibility of calculations
weaknesses: Analysis is confined to elementary descriptive statistics without inferential testing or advanced modelling
Minor slips in the discussion (e.g. omitted sign mention of negative correlation)
No effect-size or significance testing to quantify practical impact
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics is the strongest area of the exploration: computations are correct, interpretations are clear, and mathematical tools are applied competently at SL level. Although the work does not explore advanced extensions, the depth of accurate calculations and correct application throughout justifies awarding the maximum of 6 marks, especially given the need to align the total to the examinerâs final overall mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations usually precede or follow calculations, and arguments are broken into paragraphs that finish with an interpretive statement.  Despite some long sentences, the mathematical story is intelligible and structured, so communication is âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ, satisfying band 3.

Computations of slopes, correlation coefficients, means, medians, IQRs, and standard deviations are correct and sensibly interpreted.  Minor slips (range values transposed in discussion) appear but do not materially affect conclusions.  Understanding is good and mostly correct, so band 4 (âgood understanding and mostly correctâ) is justified.

The student employs multiple forms â tables, three scatter plots with regression lines, a comparative scatter, and a box-and-whisker diagram.  Each representation is generally clear and referenced in the text.  While axis scales are occasionally cramped and one figure caption repeats, the variety and pertinence of graphics demonstrate âeffective use of different formsâ consistent with band 3.

The exploration follows a recognisable flow â introduction â aim â data â analysis â conclusion â and the narrative is generally easy to follow.  Transitions between sections are usually sign-posted (e.g. âTo begin the analysis, I choseâ¦â, âNext I decidedâ¦â) and the argument develops logically from data collection to statistical comparison.  However, occasional digressions about personal viewing habits and lengthy explanations of F1 background break the logical thread.  Overall coherence is solid but not exemplary, matching the descriptor for band 3.

The creation of multiple bespoke visuals and conversion of FIA timing data into workable form indicate creativity and initiative beyond a minimal level.  Nevertheless, no novel mathematical model or sophisticated extension is attempted, so the strand fits best at band 2.

Results are analysed using several statistics, and the student discusses how fuel burn or tyre wear may confound linear trends.  This constitutes thoughtful analysis, yet it lacks rigorous quantitative correction or alternative modelling, so band 2 is appropriate.

The conclusion revisits the hypothesis and acknowledges that broader data would strengthen findings.  Some consideration of outliersâ impact is included.  While evaluation is present, it stops short of proposing detailed future work or methodological refinements; hence band 2.

The student reflects on single-race limitations, the possible bias of being a fan, and the effect of excluding safety-car laps.  These comments show meaningful but not deep reflection, aligning with band 2 (âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ).

Standard statistical notation (mean, median, IQR, r, y=mx+c) is used correctly and variables are italicised or bolded reasonably consistently.  Minor slips (mixing MM.SS and SS.MM without consistent symbol definition) and occasional informal wording (â$	ext{approx}$â) prevent full accuracy, but overall the mathematical language is appropriate and mostly consistent â matching band 3.

The rationale is rooted in the studentâs lockdown experience and F1 enthusiasm; commentary often uses first-hand language (âmy intuition suggestsâ¦â).  This demonstrates a clear personal voice and approach, yet the investigation method itself (basic statistics) is not especially innovative, placing it comfortably in band 2.

The report contains substantial descriptive passages on COVID, Netflix viewing, and general F1 commentary that do not advance the mathematical argument, and many explanations repeat earlier points (e.g. repeated justification of linear regression).  While the essential material is present, verbosity reduces conciseness and some content is only tangentially relevant.  Hence âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ (band 2) is most appropriate.

All mathematics used (unit conversion, linear regression, summary statistics) is clearly relevant to measuring pace and consistency.  However, the exploration remains limited to elementary descriptive statistics without deeper stochastic or time-series analysis.  Thus mathematics is ârelevant and used appropriatelyâ (band 3) rather than comprehensively.

Choosing a personally relevant topic, collecting raw FIA data, and designing bespoke graphs show significant evidence of independent decision-making.  The mathematical techniques themselves are standard, so the work does not reach the âoutstandingâ threshold, but band 2 (âsignificant evidenceâ) is warranted.

A clear introduction, body with subsection headings, and a summary/bibliography show good structural planning.  Figures and tables are labelled and generally placed near the text that discusses them.  Some pagination gaps and image references (e.g. â_page_11_Figure_0â) disrupt flow, but the overall skeleton (title page, rationale, methodology, results, discussion) remains evident.  This aligns with a well-organised but not âexceptionallyâ polished piece (band 3).

The tools lie squarely within SL syllabus (linear models, correlation, basic statistics).  There is no material below level, but also none extending well beyond it.  Hence the mathematics is âcommensurate with course levelâ â band 3.

annotations.comment: The aim is articulated clearly, with a focused research question comparing Verstappenâs raw pace and consistency to PÃ©rezâs in a specific race.
The explanation of spreadsheet creation and time conversion rationale is clear, demonstrating appropriate use of mathematics to prepare data.
The table header labels âTime (MM.SS)â conflict with converted âSS.MMâ data format. Ensure consistency in notation and units.
Caption lacks detail on which points were excluded as anomalies. State specific criteria for exclusion to improve transparency.
The explanation for choosing linear regression ends abruptly and leaves the rationale incomplete. Finish the sentence to clarify the choice.
Include the regression equations and corresponding r-values directly on Fig. 3 so the graph is self-contained.
Equations such as $y\approx-0.0681x+85.7$ should be enclosed in $$ for consistent LaTeX formatting.
Negative correlation coefficients appear without noting their sign in text; explicitly discuss what a negative $r$ implies for lap-time trends.
The title uses both âBrazil GPâ and later âSÃ£o Paulo Grand Prix,â causing inconsistency. Use uniform naming throughout to improve coherence and avoid reader confusion.
Engaging narrative about personal lockdown viewing habits clearly demonstrates the studentâs personal engagement and motivation behind the investigation.
Lengthy descriptions of F1 background and Netflix anecdote interrupt the mathematical focus. Trim or relocate these details to maintain logical flow.
Focusing on a single race limits generalizability. Consider expanding the dataset to multiple events to strengthen the conclusions.
The methodology does not specify the exact criteria used to exclude safety-car laps and anomalies. Define your outlier removal process explicitly.
Using official FIA data is an excellent choice, ensuring accuracy and reproducibility of results.
Noting the omission of lap 1 due to anomalies shows thoughtful reflection on data quality.
The dense paragraph describing planned analysis lacks sub-headings or bullets, reducing readability. Break into smaller sections.
The scatter plot in Fig. 2 effectively visualizes lap-time distributions for both drivers, supporting the hypothesis with clear multiple representation.
The detailed F1 technical commentary on G-forces and cockpit temperature diverts from the mathematical analysis; streamline to maintain focus.
This paragraph revisits methodological details rather than focusing on numerical findingsâcondense to concentrate on mathematical outcomes.
Bibliography entries vary in style and completeness; adopt a consistent referencing format and include all publication details.
The interpretation attributes the strong $r$-value to Verstappen but the preceding sentence discusses PÃ©rez. Correct the driver reference for clarity.
Plotting PÃ©rezâs lap times against Verstappenâs (Fig. 5) provides a creative multiple representation, facilitating direct performance comparison.
Axes were switched without explaining how this affects regression slope versus correlation strength; elaborate on the invariance of $r$ under axis swap.
The discussion acknowledges confounders like tire degradation and strategy, showing thoughtful reflection on factors beyond raw data.
The box-and-whisker diagram succinctly summarizes distribution metrics and complements scatter plots effectively.
The statistical table omits a column or footnote identifying outlier values seen in the box plot; include this to link visuals and data.
Consider calculating an effect size or significance test for the median difference to quantify the practical impact of the time gap.
Interpreting similar IQRs as equal performance is misleading given different medians; clarify that central tendency also matters.
Insightful comparison of range and standard deviation demonstrates critical thinking about trade-offs in variability measures.
Verify access-date formatting and italicize website titles in the bibliography to adhere to academic conventions.
Bibliographic sources are authoritative and relevant, strengthening the credibility and reproducibility of the exploration.
